Audit Finding No. 8

Daily collections for the year 2017 were not deposited intact.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer maintain a separate cashbook for the recording of collection in SEF and TF pursuant to Section 310 of the Local Government Code and Section 26, Volume II of the NGAS Manual to properly monitor the collections per fund and also to determine the balance of accountability per fund for a particular period.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer observed the audit recommendation set forth by the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Collections from SEF and Trust Fund were already recorded in their respective cashbook.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer improve the existing internal control system on Cash Tickets to avoid excessive requisition that would eventually lead to large number/ amount of cash tickets on hand thereby exposing it to possible loss, abuse or mismanagement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is monitoring strictly the quantity of the cash tickets on hand.
observations.reason: Requisition of cash tickets has been regulated during the year under audit.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer   adhere to the provisions of Section 48 of the MNGAS by adapting the imprest system as a method of recording the petty cash fund.
observations.reason: Imprest System is not adopted in the recording of PCF transactions.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer make sure that the amount of cash advances granted for PCF replenishment be equal to the total petty cash vouchers subject for replenishment during the period supported by Report of Disbursements.
observations.reason: The amount of cash advances granted for the replenishment of Petty Cash Fund is not equivalent to the total amount of petty cash disbursement subject for replenishment.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant be strict in the granting of Cash Advance and observe those allowable transactions that can only be availed of thru cash advance pursuant to COA Circular 97-002.
observations.reason: The Municipal Mayor was allowed to cash advance purposely to pay Hotel Accommodation and Honorarium of Speakers during the Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP) Seminar contrary to COA Circular 97-002.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management strictly comply with Item 3.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 that separates the payment and recording of Cash Advance for Wages of Casual/ Job Order from the Cash Advance for PCF to improve the proper handling, custody and disposition of PCF as well as the Payroll Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer record the payment of salaries and wages of Job Order using the cashbook of Payroll Fund
observations.reason: Examination of the Cashbooks revealed that wages of Job Order and Casuals were being charged against payroll fund.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipality adopt payment of their payroll thru bank system to avail of its advantages as mentioned in the foregoing paragraph.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still implementing the manual system in payroll operation by paying regular employees in cash.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entry to recognize the aforementioned audit disallowances in the books of accounts of the municipality in order to reflect the correct balance of the receivable account in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already recorded the Audit Disallowance to the books of accounts.
observations.reason: As per verification with Statement of Financial Position as of December 31, 2017, audit disallowances which become final and executory are already recorded as Receivable-Disallowances.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management request the proper write-off and be submitted to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with the above-cited regulations.
observations.reason: Dormant accounts still remained outstanding in the books for more than ten (10) years.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer release the amount of RPT Share due for barangay as prescribed in Section 271 (d) of the Local Government Code in order that such funds could be utilized by the barangays to finance their community development projects.
observations.reason: Verification of subsidiary ledgers disclosed that Barangay RPT Share totaling to                      P 1,462,179.78 as of December 31, 2017 remained un-released.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in granting of cash advances. Extra effort should be exerted for the immediate settlement/liquidation of the cash advances especially those granted to elected officials.
observations.reason: Verification of accounts revealed that Advances to Officers and Employees account under the General Fund had an outstanding balance of                       P 239,088.52 as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer require all employees of the municipality to comply with the minimum take home pay requirements provided in the General Appropriations Act.
observations.reason: Examination on the payment of salaries of municipal employees disclosed that the net take home pay after loan and other payroll deductions were below the  minimum net pay of Four Thousand Pesos (P 4,000.00) per month contrary to Section 47 of GAA for FY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert utmost efforts to meet the demands of timely reporting and submission of accounts.  This is to avoid undue delays that preclude the timely conduct of audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is exerting its effort to render the accounts on time.
observations.reason: The Management has improved in the submission of accounts within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We have encouraged the management to continue its duty to withhold and remit taxes.
observations.reason: For calendar year 2017, the Accounting Department did not withhold taxes from payment of honorarium to Municipal Consultants totaling                      P 713,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an inventory committee to conduct physical inventory of all property of the LGU and submit a Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) pursuant to existing auditing rules and regulations mentioned above. Thereafter, require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the book records with the inventory report.

We have recommended that management conduct physical inventory count as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2016-21 to ensure smooth transition of accountability from the outgoing to the incoming elected officials.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation expense and impairment loss on the following PPE mentioned above in order to correctly present their balances in the financial statements in consonance with PPSAS. Furthermore, the Local Chief Executive to create an appraisal committee task to determine the useful life and appraise value of the above PPE as a basis for the computation of depreciation expense and impairment loss by the Municipal Accountant.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entry to capitalized the following purchases as Office Equipment, IT Equipment and Other Machinery and Equipment to reflect the correct balance of those accounts in line with PPSAS No. 17. Likewise, we advise the General Service Officer (GSO) to prepare and issue Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) to acknowledge the receipt from the end-user and to maintain proper accountability on those assets.
We have recommended that the GSO prepare Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) to acknowledge the receipt of property and equipment for official use by the recipients from the office.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Head of the Procurement Entity  observe strictly the pertinent provisions of RA 9184 on the procurement which should be coursed thru BAC and with public bidding as the primary mode, to promote accountability, economy and transparency in the procurement process.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant examine the completeness of the supporting documents for every transactions of the Municipality before approving the same. If there are claims submitted to the Accounting Office for processing, but lacks the required supporting documents, return the DV to the claimant for proper documentation.
Moreover, the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare the BAC Resolution that will as served as a basis in determining the type of alternative method of procurement to be adopted in compliance with Section 12 of the IRR of RA 9184.
Finally, the Municipal Treasurer is advised to pay only claims that are properly approved /certified and supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

We have recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant strictly comply with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 and refrain from those expense items that are not connected with the implementation of development projects, programs and activities of the municipality. The 20% Development Fund shall be appropriated and disbursed only for the very purpose for which the fund was established.
We have recommended that the Barangay Bookkeepers of the Municipality refrain from collecting honoraria to Barangays.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant adopt the rates prescribe in DBM Circular No. 2004-05 for the payment of BAC honorarium.  Likewise, a list of successfully completed procurement projects be attach to the disbursement voucher to support the payment of BAC honorarium which will determined the reasonableness of the honorarium paid to the BAC and TWG Members as provided under DBM Circular No.2004-05A.

We have recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer and the Municipal Accountant not to allow municipal officials and employees to avail the monetization using the balance of their sick leave credits, in the absence of any proof of financial difficulty experienced by them at the time of their application pursuant to Section 23 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave.  Likewise, the Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant must refrain from utilizing appropriated funds under Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses in the payment of monetization of Leave Credits.  The funds to be utilized in the payment of monetization must come from the appropriation of Terminal Leave/Monetization under Personnel Services.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive create a group task to undertake a thorough study and evaluation on the actual workloads and manpower requirements of the Municipality for each Department/Office to minimize government spending and to avoid redundancy of work load.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly adhere with the above cited provisions governing collections and deposits of government funds. Moreover, collections are to be deposited intact not later than the next banking day in order to safeguard funds from the risk of loss, misapplication or misused.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer maintain a separate cashbook for the recording of collection in SEF and TF pursuant to Section 310 of the Local Government Code and Section 26, Volume II of the NGAS Manual to properly monitor the collections per fund and also to determine the balance of accountability per fund for a particular period.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer improve the existing internal control system on Cash Tickets to avoid excessive requisition that would eventually lead to large number/ amount of cash tickets on hand thereby exposing it to possible loss, abuse or mismanagement.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 48 of the NGAS Manual by adapting the imprest system in the recording of petty cash transactions.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer make sure that the amount of cash advances granted for PCF replenishment be equal to the total petty cash vouchers subject for replenishment during the period supported by Report of Disbursements.
Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant is advised not to allow the granting of cash advance to the Petty Cash Custodian in cases where the previous cash advance granted to her is not yet liquidated or proper accounting thereof is not made to avoid accumulation of cash advances.
Finally, the Municipal Treasurer to instruct the PCF Custodian not to pay disbursements exceeding P 15,000.00 out of the PCF, instead it shall be paid thru check.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant be strict in the granting of Cash Advance and observe those allowable transactions that can only be availed of thru cash advance pursuant to COA Circular 97-002.

We have recommended that management strictly comply with Item 3.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 that separates the payment and recording of Cash Advance for Wages of Casual/ Job Order from the Cash Advance for PCF to improve the proper handling, custody and disposition of PCF as well as the Payroll Fund.
We have recommended that Management adopt payment of their payroll thru bank system to avail of its advantages as mentioned in the foregoing paragraph.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entry to recognize the aforementioned audit disallowances in the books of accounts of the municipality in order to reflect the correct balance of the receivable account in the Statement of Financial Position.
We have recommended that Management request the proper write-off and be submitted to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with the above-cited regulations.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer release the amount of RPT Share due for barangay as prescribed in Section 271 (d) of the Local Government Code in order that such funds could be utilized by the barangays to finance their community development projects.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer require all employees of the municipality to comply with the minimum take home pay requirements provided in the General Appropriations Act.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert utmost efforts to meet the demands of timely reporting and submission of accounts.  This is to avoid undue delays that preclude the timely conduct of audit.
Since taxes are the lifeblood of the government and its prompt and certain availability is an imperious need, we encouraged the Management to continue its duty to withhold and remit taxes.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee that will conduct complete physical count of all its PPE not only under the General Fund but also properties acquired through Special Education Fund and to prepare and submit the required inventory report thereon and to reconcile the inventory report with the accounting records. 

Further, the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer should be required to prepare PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, for proper identification of PPE.
observations.managementAction: The management did not conduct its annual physical inventory of PPE as of December 31, 2016.
observations.reason:
Audit Finding No. 1.

For calendar year 2016, the Local Chief Executive did not create Inventory Committee who will conduct the annual physical inventory on PPE.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended for the Local Chief Executive to require the Inventory Committee to be headed by the Chief of the GSO to conduct inventory of all unserviceable and obsolete properties and submit a report thereon. 

Also, instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the unserviceable properties to Other Assets account to correct the valuation of the PPE account in the financial statements while expediting the disposal thereof to prevent them from further deterioration.
observations.managementAction: The management did not conduct its annual physical inventory of PPE as of December 31, 2016.
observations.reason:
Audit Finding No. 1.

For calendar year 2016, the Local Chief Executive did not create Inventory Committee who will conduct the annual physical inventory on PPE.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with Municipal Assessor prepare Real Property Tax Account Register and submit to the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the name of the taxpayers and amount due and collectible from each of them at the start of each year to serve as basis in setting-up the Real Property Tax / Special Education Tax Receivables.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality agreed to include in their Annual Budget the purchase of application software in order to generate the actual list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible.
observations.reason: As per interview with the Municipal Accountant, RPT and SEF Receivables were set-up based on estimates at the beginning of the year, amounting to P9,200,000.00 for RPT Receivables and P9,200,000.00 for SEF Receivables.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management take extra effort to demand the immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances from the concerned officials and employees pursuant to COA Circular 97-002. 

Impose sanctions prescribed in the Circular for non-compliance thereof, such as withholding of salaries of those who continuously disregard, fail or decline to comply with the demand, including filing of appropriate charges against them to compel liquidation thereof.
observations.reason:
Audit Finding No. 6.

As of December 31, 2016, the outstanding prior year’s cash advance amounts to P 409,534.43, showing a material decrease of P 1,521,679.98 compared to last year’s balance, this was the result of the Demand Letters made by the Audit Team issued to accountable officers requiring them to liquidate/refund their respective cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a detailed review of the dormant accounts and effect necessary adjustments following the guidelines provided by COA Circular No. 97-001.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will review the dormant accounts, and make proper adjustments.
observations.reason:
Audit Finding No. 5.C. 

Examination of Statement of Financial Position still reflects the dormant accounts of assets and liabilities totaling to P 1,061,153.89 remain unadjusted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to enjoined all employees of the municipality to comply with the requirements set forth under Section 48 of the General Appropriations Act (GAA) of 2015 and adopt control measures to ensure that the employee’s monthly net take home pay shall not be lower than Three Thousand Pesos                        (P 3,000.00) after deducting mandatory contributions and other obligations.
observations.managementAction: The Management has agreed to advise their employees to minimize their loan applications to avoid having a net take home pay below                      P 3,000.00.
observations.reason:
Audit Finding No. 7. 

There are still employees of the municipality whose net take home pay is below the minimum required by GAA FY 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the LGU to adopt the imprest fund system in keeping the Petty Cash Fund account to refrain  incurring excessive balances at any given period.

Replenishment of the PCF should only be equivalent to the total amount of expenditures made therefrom to avoid excessive withdrawals resulting in huge amount of the fund on hand at the end of each month as shown in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to adopt the imprest system in recording the PCF.
observations.reason:
Audit Finding No. 4.D and 4.E, 

The municipality has not yet adopted the Imprest System as a method of recording in Petty Cash Fund.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the local government unit adopt the remedies/measures provided in Section 254 of Republic Act 7160 to intensify its collection of real property taxes from delinquent taxpayers and exert more effort in the collection of stall rentals.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had planned and implemented various scheme to improve the collections of the municipality, most especially from the delinquent tax payers.
